title: श्री अष्ट दिक्पालक स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अष्ट दिक्पालक स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ अष्टदिक्पालकेभ्यो नमः।
सर्वदिक्‍रक्षकेभ्यो नमो नमः॥

पूर्वस्यां दिशि देवेन्द्रं वज्रहस्तं शचीपतिम्।
ऐरावतगजारूढं दिक्पालं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ १॥

अग्निं दक्षिणपूर्वस्यां सप्तजिह्वं हुताशनम्।
मेषवाहनमारूढं दिक्पालं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ २॥

दक्षिणस्यां दिशि यमं दण्डहस्तं यमुनाग्रजम्।
महिषवाहनारूढं दिक्पालं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ३॥

नैर्ऋत्यां राक्षसेशानं निऋऋतिं खड्गधारिणम्।
रक्षोगणपतिं वन्दे दिक्पालं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ४॥

पश्चिमस्यां दिशि वरुणं पाशहस्तं जलेश्वरम्।
मकरवाहनारूढं दिक्पालं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ५॥

वायव्यां पवनं देवं ध्वजहस्तं बलप्रदम्।
मृगवाहनमारूढं दिक्पालं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ६॥

उत्तरस्यां दिशि कुबेरं गदाहस्तं धनेश्वरम्।
नरवाहनमारूढं दिक्पालं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

ईशान्यां शूलपाणिं च ईशानं परमेश्वरम्।
वृषभवाहनारूढं दिक्पालं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ८॥

ऊर्ध्वं ब्रह्माणमीशं च हंसवाहनमव्ययम्।
चतुर्मुखं वेदविदं दिक्पालं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ९॥

अधो विष्णुं महादेवं शेषनागोपरि स्थितम्।
अनन्तशयनं धात्रीं दिक्पालं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ १०॥

इन्द्रोऽग्निर्यमो रक्षो वरुणो मारुतस्तथा।
कुबेर ईशः सर्वे मे दिक्पालाः सन्तु शान्तिदाः॥ ११॥

अष्टदिक्पालकस्तोत्रं यः पठेद् भक्तिसंयुतः।
सर्वदिक्‍भ्यो रक्षिता स्यात् वास्तुदोषं च नश्यति॥ १२॥

॥ इति श्री अष्ट दिक्पालक स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ashta Dikpalaka Stotram ||

Om Ashta-dikpalakebhyo namah |
Sarva-dik-rakshakebhyo namo namah ||

Purvasyam dishi Devendram Vajra-hastam Shachi-patim |
Airavata-gaja-arudham Dikpalam pranamamy-aham || 1 ||

Agnim dakshina-purvasyam Sapta-jihvam Hutashanam |
Mesha-vahanam-arudham Dikpalam pranamamy-aham || 2 ||

Dakshinasyam dishi Yamam Danda-hastam Yamuna-agrajam |
Mahisha-vahana-arudham Dikpalam pranamamy-aham || 3 ||

Nairrittyam Rakshaseshanam Nirrritim Khadga-dharinam |
Raksho-gana-patim vande Dikpalam pranamamy-aham || 4 ||

Pashchimasyam dishi Varunam Pasha-hastam Jaleshwaram |
Makara-vahana-arudham Dikpalam pranamamy-aham || 5 ||

Vayavyam Pavanam Devam Dhvaja-hastam Bala-pradam |
Mriga-vahanam-arudham Dikpalam pranamamy-aham || 6 ||

Uttarasyam dishi Kuberam Gada-hastam Dhaneshwaram |
Nara-vahanam-arudham Dikpalam pranamamy-aham || 7 ||

Ishanyam Shula-panim cha Ishanam Parameshwaram |
Vrishabha-vahana-arudham Dikpalam pranamamy-aham || 8 ||

Urdhvam Brahmanam-Isham cha Hamsa-vahanam-avyayam |
Chatur-mukham Veda-vidam Dikpalam pranamamy-aham || 9 ||

Adho Vishnum Mahadevam Sheshanaga-upari sthitam |
Ananta-shayanam Dhatrim Dikpalam pranamamy-aham || 10 ||

Indro-Agnir-Yamo Raksho Varuno Marutas-tatha |
Kubera Ishah sarve me Dikpalah santu shanti-dah || 11 ||

Ashta-Dikpalaka-stotram yah pathed bhakti-samyutah |
Sarva-dikbhyo rakshita syat Vastu-dosham cha nashyati || 12 ||

|| Iti Shri Ashta Dikpalaka Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: पूर्व दिशा में वज्रहस्त, शचीपति, ऐरावत पर आरूढ़ इन्द्र को प्रणाम। अग्निकोण (दक्षिण-पूर्व) में सप्तजिह्व, मेषवाहन अग्नि को प्रणाम। दक्षिण में दण्डहस्त, महिषवाहन यम को प्रणाम। नैऋत्य (दक्षिण-पश्चिम) में खड्गधारी निऋऋति को प्रणाम। पश्चिम में पाशहस्त, मकरवाहन, जलेश्वर वरुण को प्रणाम। वायव्य (उत्तर-पश्चिम) में ध्वजहस्त, मृगवाहन, बलप्रद वायु को प्रणाम। उत्तर में गदाहस्त, नरवाहन, धनेश्वर कुबेर को प्रणाम। ईशान (उत्तर-पूर्व) में शूलपाणि, वृषभवाहन, परमेश्वर ईशान (शिव) को प्रणाम। ऊर्ध्व में हंसवाहन, चतुर्मुख ब्रह्मा को और अधो में शेषनाग पर शयन करने वाले विष्णु को प्रणाम। सभी दिक्पाल शान्ति प्रदान करें। इस स्तोत्र के पाठ से सभी दिशाओं से रक्षा होती है और वास्तुदोष नष्ट होता है।
benefits: Invokes protection from all eight directions simultaneously
Traditionally recited for removal of vastu dosha from homes and buildings
Invokes the blessings of all eight directional guardians
Traditionally invoked to establish a protective shield around the devotee
Essential for Vastu Shastra remedies and corrections
Invokes the blessing of peace, prosperity and security from all sides
description: Dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the Ashta Dikpalaka Stotram (श्री अष्ट दिक्पालक स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. Rooted in Vastu Shastra and Matsya Purana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to invoke protection from all eight directions simultaneously, bestow peace, prosperity and security from all sides, and remove Vastu dosha from homes and buildings. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Sunday (Ravivar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Griha Pravesh and Diwali. At 12 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 8 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Ashta Dikpalaka Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ashta Dikpalaka Stotram in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Prayer to 8 directional deities for Vastu and protection.
relatedSearches: Ashta Dikpalaka Stotram lyrics in Sanskrit
Eight directional guardians of Hinduism with mantras
Indra Agni Yama Nirriti Varuna Vayu Kubera Ishana

seoFaqs.question: Who are the eight Dikpalakas and what directions do they govern?
seoFaqs.answer: The eight Dikpalakas are the divine guardians of the cardinal and intercardinal directions: Indra (East), Agni (South-East), Yama (South), Nirriti (South-West), Varuna (West), Vayu (North-West), Kubera (North) and Ishana (North-East). Together they are called the Asta-dik-palaka, with dik meaning direction and palaka meaning guardian. Some traditions append Brahma at the zenith and Vishnu (or Anantha) at the nadir, making a Dasa-dikpalaka set, but the standard Vastu and stotra list keeps to eight. (Sources: Wikipedia Guardians of the Directions; Templepurohit; Wisdomlib.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the relationship between the Dikpalakas and Vastu Shastra?
seoFaqs.answer: Knowledge of the Ashtadikpalakas is foundational to Vastu Shastra, the traditional Hindu science of architecture and design. Each direction inherits the qualities of its presiding deity: Indra (East) governs prosperity and authority — hence main entrances and worship rooms are auspiciously placed there; Agni (South-East) governs fire — hence the kitchen; Yama (South) is associated with restraint — hence master bedrooms; Varuna (West) with water — hence dining and reservoirs; Kubera (North) with wealth — hence safes and treasure storage; Ishana (North-East) with sanctity — hence the puja room. The Vastu Purusha Mandala formalises these placements. (Sources: Templepurohit; Exotic India Art; Vedicfolks.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the vahanas (mounts) and weapons of each Dikpalaka?
seoFaqs.answer: Indra rides the white elephant Airavata and wields the vajra (thunderbolt); Agni rides a ram and carries a flaming spear; Yama rides a buffalo and bears a danda (staff) and noose; Nirriti rides a lion or man and carries a sword; Varuna rides Makara (sea-creature) and bears the pasha (noose); Vayu rides an antelope or deer and carries a flag (dhwaja); Kubera rides a man (nara) or chariot of treasures and bears a mace and money-bag; Ishana rides Nandi-bull and bears a trishula (trident). These attributes are repeated in the dhyana shlokas of the stotram and replicated in temple sculpture across the eight outer walls of the garbhagriha. (Sources: Templepurohit; Hindu Devotional Blog; Exotic India Art.)

seoFaqs.question: When and why is the Ashta Dikpalaka Stotram chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram is recited principally at the start of any major ritual to consecrate the eight directions of the worship space — including griha pravesha (house warming), bhumi puja (ground-breaking), Vastu Shanti, Navagraha homa, marriage, upanayana and major yajnas. It is also part of the daily Sandhya-vandana sequence in some Smarta lineages, recited at sunrise, noon and sunset to acknowledge the directional protectors. Performing the eight dikpalaka homa is held to remove obstacles arising from directional doshas and to harmonise the household with cosmic order. (Sources: Vedicfolks; Sanskritdocuments; Ramana Maharshi Foundation.)

seoFaqs.question: Are the Dikpalakas the same as the Lokapalas?
seoFaqs.answer: The terms overlap substantially but differ slightly in scope. Lokapalas are 'guardians of the worlds' (loka), and the eight standard Dikpalakas are also Lokapalas in this broader sense. Some Tantric texts distinguish them by counting Lokapalas as the protectors of moral and cosmic spheres (Indra of Svarga, Yama of Pitru-loka, Varuna of the waters, Kubera of treasures), while Dikpalakas are specifically tied to spatial directions of the manifested cosmos. In daily liturgical use the two terms are interchangeable, and the same eight names appear in both lists. (Sources: Wikipedia Guardians of the Directions; Wisdomlib.)

seoFaqs.question: How are the Dikpalakas represented in temple architecture?
seoFaqs.answer: In a properly constructed Hindu temple, sculptural representations of the Ashta Dikpalakas are carved on the eight outer faces of the vimana (sanctum tower), each at the cardinal or intercardinal point they govern, riding their respective vahanas with their characteristic weapons. They also appear on the eight directional pillars of the mandapa hall and as guardian deities flanking the gopuram. Their inclusion is mandatory in Agama-compliant temples (Vaikhanasa, Pancharatra and Shaiva-Siddhanta agamas all prescribe their placement), and the consecration of the temple includes a separate Ashtadikpalaka pratishtha. (Sources: Exotic India Art; Templepurohit.)
